findings.description: count_paths recurses with no cycle detection and no memoization, so an attacker-supplied cyclic graph causes unbounded recursion (RecursionError/stack exhaustion) and even a valid diamond-shaped DAG yields exponential-time path counting — a resource-exhaustion DoS (CWE-674/CWE-400).

reachable() enqueues every neighbor with no visited guard, so on any cyclic graph the queue grows without bound and the loop never terminates, exhausting memory/CPU (CWE-400 resource exhaustion / CWE-835 infinite loop) on untrusted graph input.

The BFS uses `seen` only to record visited nodes, never to prune enqueueing, making traversal non-terminating and incorrect on cyclic graphs; the standard `if neighbor not in seen` guard before append is missing (logic root cause of the reachable() DoS; defer full logic treatment to the logic critic).

The module imposes no caps on graph size, recursion depth, or traversal work, so it provides no resource limits when fed untrusted/attacker-influenced graph data — the umbrella mitigation for the count_paths/reachable DoS vectors (CWE-770 allocation/work without limits).

topological_order assumes every edge endpoint exists as an indegree key; this holds through the current public API (add_edge registers both endpoints), but the unchecked `indegree[dst] += 1` would raise an unhandled KeyError (availability) if that internal invariant were ever broken — low-confidence defensive note.
